entry.title: Combined summary - Hourglass V2 Update
entry.summary: The updated Hourglass proposal aims to mitigate the risks of mass liquidation of Pay-to-PubKey (P2PK) funds within the Bitcoin network by introducing a limitation on the amount of Bitcoin that can be spent from these outputs, capping it at one Bitcoin per block. This measure is designed to enhance the security and stability of the network by preventing market disruptions that could result from large-scale simultaneous liquidations. The specifics of these adjustments and their rationale are detailed in the [Hourglass Proposal V2](https://github.com/cryptoquick/bips/blob/hourglass-v2/bip-hourglass-v2.mediawiki), which was revised based on community feedback. The initial discussions and community's response to the Hourglass proposal, highlighting the engagement and collaborative efforts to refine the proposal, can be revisited through the [Bitcoin Development Mailing List Discussion](https://groups.google.com/g/bitcoindev/c/zmg3U117aNc/m/lDCMs9j7EAAJ). Critics of implementing in-protocol plunge protection mechanisms argue that such restrictions contradict Bitcoin's foundational principles by limiting users' ability to spend their coins freely. This viewpoint stresses the importance of preserving Bitcoin's integrity as an unregulated and decentralized peer-to-peer electronic cash system. Despite these concerns, the proposal underscores the Bitcoin community's authority to protect the network against malicious entities through consensus-driven preventive measures. The discourse also explores the potential for a Zero-Knowledge (ZK) quantum-safe spending option to address the migration of UTXOs to quantum-resistant scripts, acknowledging the technical hurdles faced by owners of older P2PK outputs. Additionally, the conversation delves into adjusting bitcoin distribution in response to quantum threats by proposing a reduction in the daily issuance of bitcoins, thereby decreasing the incentive for quantum attacks. This approach includes tying the release of P2PK coins directly to the coinbase reward structure to ensure a controlled and proportional introduction of new coins. Further analysis suggests that concerns remain regarding the potential manipulation of this system by large miners, emphasizing the need for solutions that align with cryptocurrency's core principles while addressing quantum vulnerabilities. The arbitrary assignment of 1 BTC per block as a limit for spending from P2PK outputs is deemed a conceptual method to communicate and mitigate quantum-related fears, balancing the liquidation speed of the entire set against the waiting period for original keyholders to access their P2PK coins.
